overview: Prison Shops is a control archetype that slows the game to a crawl by taxing opponent spells and combat damage. It relies on artifact-based stax pieces and card advantage to outlast opponents in the Vintage format. The deck aims to stabilize early, then grind down the opponent through attrition or decisive artifact creature attacks.
win_conditions: Opponent running out of resources due to spell and combat taxes

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction or artifact lands
Hands containing Sphere of Resistance or Lodestone Golem
Hands with multiple lands and a win condition
Keep hands with 1-2 lands, Golos or Tireless Pilgrim, and at least one lock piece (Lodestone Golem, Sphere of Resistance).
Keep hands with acceleration + a lock piece, or Golos + Pilgrim + 2 lands.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or early answers
Mulligan hands with only lock pieces and no mana sources, multiple lands with no early plays, or hands lacking a way to cast turn-1/2 threats.

early_game: Play artifact lands and cheap stax pieces
Use Sphere of Resistance to tax opponent's early spells
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a mana artifact or Golos/Pilgrim if mana allows.
If you have acceleration, cast Lodestone Golem or Sphere of Resistance.
Turn 2: Use Golos/Pilgrim to generate mana, cast additional lock pieces, or start setting up the win condition.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetching/playing lands that tap for blue or colorless, ensuring you can cast blue spells and artifacts.
mid_game: Deploy Lodestone Golem to tax combat and gain card advantage
Search for Golos, Tireless Pilgrim to stabilize and draw cards
Maintain board presence while digging for answers
Always prioritize casting lock pieces over drawing cards.
Use Golos/Pilgrim to generate mana for win conditions, not just more locks.
Manage hand by keeping answers for key threats.
Graveyard is usually not a primary focus unless facing Dredge.
Commit threats only when the board is locked down.
late_game: Use Golos to draw into finishers or additional stax
Attack with artifact creatures to bypass opponent's defenses
Grind opponent out of resources through continuous taxes
Golos + Tireless Pilgrim generating massive mana to cast Emrakul, Aeons Torn, Ugin, or a combo finisher.
Alternatively, attacking with a massive threat or using a combo like Urza's Saga.
When you have locked the opponent out of relevant interactions and have enough mana to cast a win condition or attack for lethal.
Also commit when the opponent has no removal for your lock pieces.

key_cards.name: Lodestone Golem
key_cards.role: Combat tax and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Play early to force opponent to pay life or lose creatures, generating value and slowing aggressive strategies.

key_cards.name: Sphere of Resistance
key_cards.notes: Increases spell costs to disrupt combo and aggro, while making artifact creatures cheaper to cast.

key_cards.name: Golos, Tireless Pilgrim
key_cards.notes: Search for artifact lands or stax pieces, then attack to draw cards and close out the game.

sequencing_tips: Hold Sphere of Resistance until opponent casts key spells to maximize tax impact
Play Lodestone Golem before opponent commits multiple creatures to the board
Use Golos to fetch lands when mana is tight, or fetch stax when board is clear
Common mistakes: Overcommitting Golos/Pilgrim too early without a win condition, failing to lock the opponent's mana, or ignoring graveyard strategies.
Unique interactions: Golos + Pilgrim can generate massive mana if you have enough artifacts.
Sphere of Resistance + Lodestone Golem can make spells uncastable.
Watch out for artifact destruction and graveyard recursion.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Lodestone Golem and Sphere of Resistance to tax attacks and spells, forcing opponent to overextend.
Use Sphere of Resistance to disrupt casting, then deploy Lodestone Golem to stop any creature-based finishes.
Race to deploy Golos and stax pieces before opponent establishes their own board control.
Tax their value generators with Sphere and Golem, then out-grind them with Golos.
Disrupt their early land drops with Sphere, then tax their big spells with Lodestone Golem.

Use cheap lock pieces that don't need to be countered, or cast them in response to counterspells. Use counterspells yourself if in the deck. Rely on the fact that lock pieces are often cheap or free with acceleration.
Win the resource war. Prioritize locking their mana and hand. Use counterspells and discard to disrupt their setup. Golos/Pilgrim sequencing is key to out-generating their mana.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard recursion (Dredge), mana acceleration (Oath), or artifact destruction (Initiative/Control).
Key tells: Opponent playing multiple lands, casting cheap creatures, or targeting your artifacts.
